citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
In 2025, the facility did not use any method to diagnose or screen for fecal intestinal parasites on their 5 adult fallow deer
that are housed outdoors.
The facility's program of veterinary care specifies that the fallow deer's stools shall be tested "at least yearly" for fecal
intestinal parasites. According to medical records, the last time the facility conducted such fecal testing on their fallow
deer was November 19, 2024, over 18 months prior to the inspection.
Regular fecal parasite testing facilitates prompt identification and treatment of intestinal parasites. Without regular testing,
parasites might go undiagnosed and untreated, which can affect the health and well-being of the deer.
Correct by performing fecal testing on the fallow deer in accordance with your program of veterinary care and by following
all other instructions from your attending veterinarian in order to facilitate the prevention, control, diagnosis, and treatment
of diseases and injuries.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class C license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by August 1, 2026, or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
In 2025, the facility did not use any method to diagnose or screen for fecal intestinal parasites on their 5 adult fallow deer 
that are housed outdoors. 
 
The facility's program of veterinary care specifies that the fallow deer's stools shall be tested "at least yearly" for fecal 
intestinal parasites. According to medical records, the last time the facility conducted such fecal testing on their fallow 
deer was November 19, 2024, over 18 months prior to the inspection. 
 
Regular fecal parasite testing facilitates prompt identification and treatment of intestinal parasites. Without regular testing, 
parasites might go undiagnosed and untreated, which can affect the health and well-being of the deer.  
 
Correct by performing fecal testing on the fallow deer in accordance with your program of veterinary care and by following 
all other instructions from your attending veterinarian in order to facilitate the prevention, control, diagnosis, and treatment 
of diseases and injuries. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class C license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by August 1, 2026, or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
